intent:
On a park bench, the narrator sits beside mother (母) while she reads. The narrator is not interested in the book and looks instead under the bench. A small paper is there. Mother takes that paper, recognizes it as bearing a friend's name. Anchor object 紙 changes from invisible-under-bench → in narrator's hand → in mother's hand → recognized.

PREMISE CONTRACT (story 6)
  one_sentence_event:    Narrator finds a paper under the park bench and gives it to mother, who recognizes it as bearing a friend's name.
  change_of_state:       at s0, narrator and mother sit on the bench, paper unseen; at closer, mother holds the paper and the narrator recognizes the friend's name on it. Object location AND knowledge both change.
  scene_novelty_claim:   I am NOT writing a classroom (s4), kitchen (s3), or station (s5) story; this is the first park_bench scene.
  anchor_novelty_claim:  Anchor is paper (紙). 紙 was introduced as W00040 in story 4 as a writing surface; here it returns as a found object, never previously the anchor.
  closer_shape_claim:    Closer shape: narrator-internal recognition with copula+ね ("…だね"). NOT a noun-pile, NOT a [N1]は[N2]を[V] mirror of stories 4–5, NOT a 「…」と言います quote.
  why_not_filler:        Mother earns her slot via the が-but contrast (she reads, narrator doesn't) AND by transferring the paper (s5 receives it). The bench earns its slot as the spatial hider of the paper. The paper earns each appearance: s4 surprise, s5 transfer, s6 recognition. その in s5 is anaphoric back to s4's 紙 — not decoration.
  obligations_absorbed:  must-reinforce G024_da + G034_ne_confirm absorbed in s6 ("友達の名前だね"). Grammar floor (≥2): G032_demo (s4) + G043_kosoado_pre_nominal (s5 via その). Auto-tagged G049_ga_but in s3 lands as a third intro at post-ship attribution time. No must-reinforce vocab is flagged.

FORBIDDEN THIS STORY (per pipeline/tools/forbid.py 6):
  scene_class    (last 3): classroom, kitchen, station — using park_bench (fresh)
  anchor_object  (last 5): apple, egg, pencil, tea_cup, umbrella — using paper (fresh; new role)
  closer_shape   (last 3): noun-WA-noun and noun-WA-noun-DE-noun-NO-noun-WO-noun and noun-WA-noun-WO-noun — using narrator-realization-with-だね (fresh)
  opening_seqs   (last 3): 朝/台所/卵 ; 私/学校/います ; 私/母/駅 — using 公園/椅子/母 (no exact 3-token overlap; "母" appears at position 3 not 2)

§G OVERRIDE USED (1/1): seed_plan vocab list calls for 祖母 (grandmother). To fit grammar_min=2 within mint_budget.max=7, dropped 祖母 in favor of 母 (existing W00005). The narrator–parent dynamic is preserved; the seed plan's spatial/sit-down/reading anchors are kept intact (公園, 椅子, 下, 座る, 本, 読む). Override consumed; no further deviations possible this story.

sentences.jp: 公園の椅子に母と私が座ります。
sentences.en: Mother and I sit on a bench in the park.

sentences.jp: 私は本を読みませんが、椅子の下を見ます。
sentences.en: I do not read the book, but I look under the bench.

sentences.jp: 椅子の下に小さい紙があります。
sentences.en: Under the bench, there is a small paper.

sentences.jp: その紙を母に見せます。
sentences.en: I show that paper to mother.
It's a friend's name, isn't it.
